validation: Review used only local repository files, local command output, and the issue text supplied in the prompt; no network access was used.
Tracked diff reviewed with git status --short, git diff --stat, git diff --name-only, and targeted git diff output.
Untracked issue files under docs/validation/local_codex_runner/issue-184/, sdk/python/src/attestplane/conformance/, tests/conformance/, and tests/sdk/ were reviewed directly from the local workspace.
No release gate was weakened. The fixture hash helper still verifies the conformance corpus, and the updated lockfile matches the local corpus hash set.
No severity was lowered. The new negative vectors still assert rejection codes and pointers for the canonicalization edge cases.
No secrets were leaked or logged. The added fixtures are synthetic test data only.
No publish, tag, merge, package publish, or PyPI push logic was modified by the diff.
No key tests were deleted. The diff adds dedicated negative-vector coverage and a compatibility shim.

Behavior is covered by local evidence: pytest -q tests/conformance/test_negative_vectors.py tests/canonicalization/test_canonicalization_properties.py tests/sdk/test_canonicalization_property.py passed with 175 tests.
Behavior is covered by local evidence: PYTHONPATH=sdk/python/src python -m attestplane.conformance.run --negative passed and printed all nine versioned negative vectors.
Fixture-lock evidence: bash scripts/check-fixture-hashes.sh passed with 'Conformance fixtures: 33 files, all canonical hashes match'.
residual_risks: This review was scoped to the current local diff and focused conformance validation, not a full repository gate.
The new versioned negative corpus is local and static; downstream consumers still need to adopt the new runner and vector directory in their own workflows.
